basic_details.job_title: Tax Policy Research Unit Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline
basic_details.short_information: The Tax Policy Research Unit has officially announced the Research Fellow Recruitment 2026 with 2 openings on a contractual basis. Candidates who meet the maximum age limit of 32 years and possess a B.Tech in the relevant discipline or a Master's degree in Economics, Commerce, Business Economics, Finance, or Business Administration along with the prescribed programming/research experience are eligible to apply. Interested candidates can submit their offline applications on or before 31 July 2026. The official notification and application details are provided below.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 32 years for all posts.

qualification.education_qualification: B Tech. in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics and Telecommunications and related streams, Master's degree in Economics/Commerce/Business Economics/Finance/Business Administration, One to five years of programming/research experience, Experience with large databases and tools like Python, R, STATA, SPSS, E-Views
qualification.details: Research Fellow (Information Technology): B Tech. in Computer Science, Information Technology, Electronics and Telecommunications and related streams; One to five years of programming experience; The ability and the experience to handle large databases; Thorough knowledge and programming ability in Python or R. Research Fellow (Economics): Master's degree in Economics/Commerce/Business Economics/Finance/Business Administration; One to five years of research and report writing experience or equivalent professional experience; The ability and the experience to handle large databases; Experience with some econometric and statistical tools like E-Views, STATA, SPSS, R etc.

selection_process: Please refer to the official notification for selection process details.
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for Tax Policy Research Unit Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: https://dor.gov.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 31-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

Research Fellow (Economics): Master's degree in Economics/Commerce/Business Economics/Finance/Business Administration; One to five years of research and report writing experience or equivalent professional experience; The ability and the experience to handle large databases; Experience with some econometric and statistical tools like E-Views, STATA, SPSS, R etc.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to apply for Tax Policy Research Unit Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below. Read the official notification carefully before applying. Download the application form from the official website: https://dor.gov.in. Fill the application form carefully with correct information. Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates. Check all details carefully before submission. Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed. Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 31-07-2026. Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

content_sections.heading: Tax Policy Research Unit Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Tax Policy Research Unit Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application for the Research Fellow posts is 31st July 2026. Ensure your application reaches the designated address before this date.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the Research Fellow position?
faq.answer: There are a total of 2 vacancies for the Research Fellow position, with one each for Information Technology and Economics specializations.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for the Research Fellow (Information Technology) post?
faq.answer: For the Research Fellow (Information Technology) post, candidates need a B.Tech in Computer Science, IT, Electronics, or related streams, along with programming experience and proficiency in Python or R.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Research Fellow posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all Research Fellow posts is 32 years. Age relaxation details, if any, should be checked in the official notification.

faq.question: Q5. What is the monthly salary for a Research Fellow at the Tax Policy Research Unit?
faq.answer: Selected candidates for the Research Fellow position will receive a consolidated monthly salary of Rs. 70,000.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application mode for the Tax Policy Research Unit Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application mode for this recruitment is offline. Candidates need to send their filled application forms to the official address mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q8. What kind of experience is required for the Research Fellow (Economics) post?
faq.answer: For the Research Fellow (Economics) post, candidates require one to five years of research and report writing experience or equivalent professional experience, along with database handling skills and knowledge of statistical tools.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I find the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official notification and application form can be downloaded from the official website of the Department of Revenue (dor.gov.in), and links are provided in the Important Links section of this article.

faq.question: Q10. What is the job type for these Research Fellow positions?
faq.answer: The Research Fellow positions are contractual in nature, as per the official notification released by the Tax Policy Research Unit.
